reviews.review_description: Journey to Impossible delivers an immersive simulation of NASA’s historic Moon mission. Pilot the command and lunar modules, manage realistic spacecraft systems, and feel the thrill of space exploration first-hand in VR. Highly recommended for anyone fascinated by history, space, or interactive learning experiences.

JOurNey to iMpOssible = ONMO = Moon
Embedded anagram.

A standing rather than seated VR experience. A deeper dive than Apollo 11 VR.
The need to steer and control resulted in a failed mission every time. This takes a few times to get it.
I prefer the experience where a person doesn't have to do all that.
The lighting of the initial shot of the rocket at Cape Canaveral was very poor and dim. I just about couldn't see the rocket at all although it looks good in the trailer.


Apollo 11 is comparable to this. No better no worse. Apollo 11 had slightly sharper graphics, no lighting issues and a passive viewer experience that doesn't require a lot of effort.

Its worth the money.
Faber Courtial Into Space, now that's smoking! Faber Courtial Into Space is absolutely mind blowing.
When it comes to mind blowing, this app is no slouch. 

If you are getting mission failed each time, every time, go to the menu and you can experience this one chapter at a time.

Very mind blowing! Leaving the lunar lander directly and stepping on to the moon was a mind blowing experience!  
Stepping on to the moon in VR was a life changing experience. It's like being born but without a mother waiting with a knife to abort them! One only has to deal with zero degrees Kelvin if something goes wrong and the pressurized space suit bursts. 
This app and Apollo 11 and Into Space complement each other so much that each is incomplete without the others. 
The app provides a complete structural understanding. The astronauts left through not even a door but a square hatch on the floor which opened to a ladder. That's so bare bones, it's Spartan. 
Spartan fare for a Tartan queer. 
In future public moon mission even those in economy class flying on coach wouldn't be expected to leave through a hatch in the floor.

reviews.review_title: Like Living Through History in VR
reviews.review_description: Amazing game. The docking part reminded me of Interstellar, and stepping onto the moon in VR was unforgettable. It feels like more than just a game, it’s like living through history. I highly recommend it to anyone who loves space or realistic VR 💯❤️
If anyone is wondering if the photo are real it is good just has a long beginning would recommend they just need to add a skip button
Felt like being part of Apollo team, docking and landing on the moon in VR was just incredible.
The visuals and sound made me feel like I was really out there in space, such a surreal experience.
Learned about space while actually living it in VR, felt both fun and meaningful
